text:
Market today: Cautious but tech-positive. SK Hynix surging 12% on Micron's blowout earnings is the real driver — semiconductor demand is being confirmed right now, which directly lifts your chip-heavy book. Hormuz tensions easing removes an oil-shock tail risk.

Your holdings: PLTR is down 12.77% and flagged SELL — no catalyst in today's news to save it, and on margin a continued slide compounds fast. AVGO is your largest position, down 12.45% and flagged TRIM — Micron's strong earnings could offer a brief bounce but that's your exit window, not a reason to hold full size. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no track record — tiny moves in the underlying can be violent; margin makes this dangerous at size.

Watch: 1) Whether AVGO catches a Micron/Hynix sympathy bounce today — that's your trim opportunity. 2) SPAL's stop at $19 — it's already down 14.5% and it's a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily.

Bottom line: Use any chip-sector pop from Micron euphoria to reduce your two biggest losers before the bounce fades.
